predictions.title: Local-first coding agents standardize cloud-overflow as default by Q3 2026
predictions.body: **Acceleration of the "local-first, cloud-overflow" inversion.** Qwen3.6-35B-A3B's 73.4% on SWE-bench Verified and Bonsai 1.7B's 674 tok/s on CUDA push edge implementations into practical territory. By Q3 2026, individual / SMB coding agents will standardize on "local by default, cloud as overflow." In plain language: In plain language: In plain language: In plain language:

reasoning.because: Qwen3.6-35B-A3B hits 73.4% on SWE-bench Verified and Bonsai 1.7B reaches 674 tok/s on CUDA.
reasoning.given: Edge-runnable models now match useful coding-agent quality bars, while cloud inference stays metered and latency-bound.
reasoning.so_that: Individual and SMB coding-agent stacks default to local execution, with cloud reserved for overflow and hard tasks.
reasoning.landing: By Q3 2026; individual + SMB coding-agent tooling.
reasoning.plain_language: Small coding AIs that run on your own computer are now good and fast enough that most people will use those first and only call the cloud when needed.

predictions.summary: Qwen3.6-35B-A3B's 73.4% on SWE-bench Verified and Bonsai 1.7B's 674 tok/s on CUDA push edge coding agents into practical territory. By Q3 2026, individual and SMB coding-agent stacks are expected to standardize on local-by-default execution, with cloud reserved for overflow.

predictions.title: Agent-in-the-Loop Secret Exfiltration tops CIS / OWASP risk lists by Q3 2026
predictions.body: **Secret leakage through CI / agents becomes the next battleground.** GitHub Actions indirect prompt injection PoCs reproduce across Claude Code, Gemini CLI, and Copilot — three vendors at once. Through Q2-Q3 2026, a new supply-chain security category, "Agent-in-the-Loop Secret Exfiltration," will rise to the top of CIS / OWASP risk lists. In plain language: In plain language: In plain language: In plain language:

reasoning.because: GitHub Actions indirect prompt injection PoCs reproduce across Claude Code, Gemini CLI, and Copilot at once.
reasoning.given: CI runners hand coding agents privileged access to secrets, tokens, and deploy keys via untrusted PR/issue text.
reasoning.so_that: A new supply-chain category, Agent-in-the-Loop Secret Exfiltration, climbs to the top of CIS / OWASP risk lists.
reasoning.landing: Through Q2-Q3 2026; CIS / OWASP top-list entries.
reasoning.plain_language: AI coding helpers can be tricked into leaking passwords from build servers. Soon, security guides will list this as a top risk to fix.

predictions.summary: GitHub Actions indirect prompt injection proofs-of-concept reproduce across Claude Code, Gemini CLI, and Copilot in the same window. Through Q2-Q3 2026, a new supply-chain category, Agent-in-the-Loop Secret Exfiltration, is expected to rise to the top of CIS and OWASP risk lists.

predictions.title: Headless Everything standardizes via MCP/Agent Registry by H2 2026
predictions.body: **"Headless Everything" architecture standardizes.** Simon Willison's Apr 19 thesis — that personal AI is faster and more reliable hitting headless services than driving a GUI — frames an axis against browser-operating agents (Claude in Chrome, etc.). By H2 2026, expect a "headless-preferred" metadata standard on MCP / Agent Registry surfaces. In plain language: In plain language: In plain language: In plain language:

reasoning.because: Simon Willison's Apr 19 thesis argues headless service calls beat GUI-driving for personal AI speed and reliability.
reasoning.given: Browser-operating agents (Claude in Chrome, etc.) are slower and more brittle than direct API calls into headless services.
reasoning.so_that: MCP / Agent Registry surfaces add a 'headless-preferred' metadata field so agents pick the headless path by default.
reasoning.landing: By H2 2026; visible on MCP / Agent Registry server metadata.
reasoning.plain_language: Agents that call services directly are faster and break less than ones that click around web pages, so registries will start labeling which path to prefer.

predictions.summary: Simon Willison's Apr 19 thesis frames headless service calls as faster and more reliable than GUI-driving for personal AI, setting up an axis against browser-operating agents like Claude in Chrome. By H2 2026, MCP and Agent Registry surfaces are expected to carry a 'headless-preferred' metadata standard.
